description_long: This government building features a large, rectangular footprint with a prominent central block and two extended wings, forming a T-shaped layout. The exterior is characterized by a grid of evenly spaced windows, suggesting a multi-story structure with ample natural light. The facade is minimalist, with clean lines and a uniform, light gray color scheme, indicative of modern institutional architecture. The building is elevated on a series of columns or stilts, creating an open ground level that may serve as a sheltered walkway or entry plaza. The roof is flat and unadorned, with no visible ornamentation or rooftop structures. The overall design emphasizes functionality, accessibility, and a sense of openness, suitable for a civic or administrative purpose.
description: A large, T-shaped government building with a grid of windows, flat roof, and elevated on columns, featuring a minimalist modern design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the central block and both wings, with the open ground level and grid windows clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlights the depth of the wings and the open space beneath the building, revealing more of the side facade.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the full width of the building and the uniform window pattern, with the central block extending forward.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view emphasizes the other wing and the open ground level, showing the building's symmetry and structure.
attribute_public_entrance_design: The main public entrance is located beneath the central block, accessible via a wide, open area supported by columns. The entrance is spacious and unobstructed, designed for easy access and high foot traffic, with large glass doors and no visible steps or barriers.
